message.content: Adversaries may exploit Microsoft Office add-ins to maintain persistence on compromised systems. These add-ins, which enhance Office program functionality, include Word/Excel add-in Libraries (WLL/XLL), VBA add-ins, COM add-ins, automation add-ins, VBA Editor (VBE), Visual Studio Tools for Office (VSTO) add-ins, and Outlook add-ins. By configuring these add-ins to execute code upon Office application startup, attackers can ensure their malicious code runs each time the application is launched. Notable examples include Bisonal, which uses a .wll extension in the Word startup directory, and Naikon, which employs the RoyalRoad exploit builder to drop a loader into the Word Startup folder. Mitigation strategies involve restricting add-in execution through Group Policy, monitoring and controlling the installation of add-ins, and regularly auditing Office application startup folders for unauthorized files.
